name: ARA Research Component Catalog
generation_contract: Agents emit ARA DSL or structured intent that maps to this catalog; they must not invent raw HTML classes.
The compiler and writer validate the rendered fragment against this catalog before publishing.
Dashboard JavaScript may progressively enhance catalog primitives, but author HTML never includes script, style, iframe, svg, event handlers, or inline style.

components.intent: Article root and logical section grouping.
components.default_when: Every fragment article starts with ara-doc; use ara-section for grouped article sections.

components.intent: Article identity and opening framing.
components.default_when: Use display for the article title, deck for a concise thesis, and lede for the opening paragraph.

components.intent: Scannable hierarchy and deep-link anchors.
components.default_when: Use numbered ara-h2 sections for the main argument, ara-h3/ara-h4 for subdivisions, and divider for strong breaks.

components.intent: Boxed aside for risk, confirmation, caveat, or methodology.
components.default_when: Use sparingly for information that changes confidence or interpretation.

components.intent: Pull quote with optional attribution.
components.default_when: Use for one sentence worth slowing down for.

components.intent: Large serif statement on a periwinkle panel — the McKinsey inside-cover thesis line.
components.default_when: Use once or twice per article for the single thesis worth a full beat. Not for ordinary emphasis.

components.intent: Small gray sans Note / Source lines beneath an exhibit, or standalone in prose.
components.default_when: Use for exhibit footnotes (methodology caveats, data provenance) or any small-print attribution line.

components.intent: Framed McKinsey exhibit: numbered eyebrow, title, units subtitle, a nested chart/figure/table, Note/Source, and an ARA wordmark.
components.default_when: Use to present any chart or figure as a formal, attributed exhibit. The inner chart's own border and watermark are suppressed so the frame and wordmark are never doubled.
components.example: :::exhibit(num="Exhibit 1", title="Revenue by segment", subtitle="$ billion", source="Company filings")

components.intent: Two-column body for dense prose; collapses to one column on narrow screens.
components.default_when: Use for a long, dense prose passage that benefits from a newspaper-style two-up measure. Do not put charts or wide blocks inside.

components.intent: Circular percentage-change marker (the McKinsey +N% / -N% bubble).
components.default_when: Use inline beside a figure or label to mark a delta. Sign infers direction (royal=up, navy=down).

components.intent: Image, caption, and source credit.
components.default_when: Use for cited visuals that help inspect the subject, not decoration.

components.intent: Compact facts and metadata.
components.default_when: Use for company facts, paper metadata, or at-a-glance attributes.

components.intent: Headline numeric grid.
components.default_when: Use for the 2-4 numbers the reader must remember.

components.intent: Comparison or ranked tabular data.
components.default_when: Use when exact row/column comparison matters more than visual proportion.

components.intent: Three-card min/max/subject comparison.
components.default_when: Use to show where the subject sits within a distribution.

components.intent: Dense ranked rows with proportional bars.
components.default_when: Use instead of a table for long ranked comparisons.

components.intent: Single-value proportional bars.
components.default_when: Use for a short set of comparable percentages or normalized magnitudes.

components.intent: One segmented proportional bar with legend.
components.default_when: Use for a single composition that sums to roughly 100.

components.intent: Many labeled segmented bars sharing one legend.
components.default_when: Use for categorical breakdowns across several entities.

components.intent: Inline mini trend with tooltip.
components.default_when: Use inside prose for small directional trend context.

components.intent: Full SVG line chart generated by the dashboard runtime.
components.default_when: Use for time series or ordered numeric series.

components.intent: Axis-bearing vertical or horizontal bar chart generated by the dashboard runtime.
components.default_when: Use when values need a real numeric axis rather than normalized data-pct bars.

components.intent: Proportional ring with legend and segment tooltips.
components.default_when: Use for part-of-whole composition with 3-7 segments.

components.intent: Two-period slopegraph with change tooltips.
components.default_when: Use for before/after change across several entities.

components.intent: Live TradingView chart placeholder enhanced by the dashboard runtime.
components.default_when: Use only when live market data is more important than reproducible as-of-date data.

components.intent: Vertical chronological log.
components.default_when: Use when date order carries semantic weight.

components.intent: Repeated glyph count for ratio-at-a-glance comparison.
components.default_when: Use for small-count ratios where repeated marks are easier than charts.

components.intent: Inline emphasis, citations, chips, highlights, and status dots.
components.default_when: Use to anchor entities, cite claims, flag state, or label topics inside prose.
